step_by_step_reasoning: We identify the main components from the whiteboard and transcript. The user calls into Amazon Connect. Connect triggers a data dip via a Lambda to Salesforce CRM (represented by Salesforce box and Lambda). Another Lambda handles Lex/natural language integration. Top S3 bucket stores contact center events, queried by Athena. Bottom S3 bucket stores call recordings, which are processed by Transcribe and analyzed by Comprehend.

graph.name: Intuit Global Contact Center Architecture
graph.notes: A serverless, scalable contact center architecture built on Amazon Connect. It utilizes automated CRM lookups, real-time analytics, and AI-driven post-call sentiment analysis.

nodes.name: Users
nodes.notes: Customers calling into the contact center.

nodes.name: Amazon Connect
nodes.notes: Cloud contact center service orchestrating voice calls and routing.

nodes.name: CRM Lambda
nodes.notes: WORKLOAD_PEEK: Executes data dips into Salesforce to retrieve user context.

nodes.name: Salesforce CRM
nodes.notes: External CRM database containing customer records.

nodes.name: Integration Lambda
nodes.notes: Integrates natural language processing and customer steering.

nodes.name: Event S3 Bucket
nodes.notes: DATA_PEEK: Stores real-time contact center event logs.

nodes.name: Athena
nodes.notes: Queries event data for real-time dashboards.

nodes.name: Transcribe
nodes.notes: Generates text transcriptions of voice calls.

nodes.name: Comprehend
nodes.notes: Performs sentiment analysis on transcribed text.
